openingThird.focus: Keep the cells and first interrogation bound to masonry, iron, torchlight, and the vault's physical bleed into the dungeon.

middleThird.focus: Prevent the tribunal from flattening into legal summary by attaching every prosecution, exhibit, and witness turn to benches, chains, papers, rails, breath, and room reaction.

finalThird.focus: Keep the verdict, cell return, and Lor'themar contact anchored in bars, dust, cadence, and vault pressure so the chapter closes on corridor fact instead of abstract consequence.

transformationMoves: Replace atmospheric or clever phrasing with local carriers such as mortar seams, ward paste, velvet nap, rails, cuffs, and flagstone response.
Anchor summary runs to procedure or body pressure: page turns, glove leather, pen scratches, rails, chain pulls, benches shifting.
Convert emotional or institutional abstractions into room events with nearby human reaction.
Keep dialogue grounded by pairing long runs with objects, posture, masonry, bars, or contact beats that reveal pressure instead of filler motion.
Use Sylvanas's POV filter for architecture, rank, gait, and threat-read rather than neutral courtroom description.

scenes.title: The Cells
scenes.biggestGap: Cold and testimony occasionally slip into atmospheric or list-like phrasing instead of staying attached to mortar, irons, stool, board, and flagstone.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Dungeon cells beneath the tribunal wing, with the evidence vault above bleeding cold down the stairwell.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Mortar seams, floor chill through palm and bone, clerk's board on his knees, stool legs, lock teeth, chain against stone.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas inventories space, sound, and official posture because counting is the only discipline left.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Questions and wall-talk stay tied to kneeling at the masonry, the clerk's pen, and the prisoner hardware.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The blade's effect returns as a physical tick in the stone and a colder floor, not a generalized sense of menace.

scenes.title: The Trial
scenes.biggestGap: The room, Ashcombe's opening, the exhibit, and Uther's testimony all risk slipping into polished summary unless bench, chain, page, rail, breath, and ward detail stay active.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Ceremonial tribunal chamber built for monarchy and public order, entered under guard from service stairs.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Chain weight, glove leather, paper edges, chest clasps, blackening ward paste, chair arms, witness rail grain.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas reads who owns consequence in the room, tracking Thornton, Ashcombe, Uther, and the blade as threat surfaces.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Every prosecution and witness run must stay pinned to page turns, bench reaction, mage handling, or hands on wood.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The scene leaves not on abstract legal force but on bodies, chains, and the visible effect of the room accepting the frame.

scenes.title: The Betrayals
scenes.biggestGap: Antonidas, Vereesa, and the verdict can blur into institutional idea-work unless each failure lands through visible bodies, cloth, vellum, seating, and room recoil.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Same tribunal chamber, now narrowed around the witness chair, gallery rows, and the center table with the blade.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Twisted cuffs, wax seal, robe cloth, witness-box emptiness, ledger scratch, chain tug after verdict.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas judges betrayal by who stands, who does not, and who can still act after the room shows its teeth.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Mentor objection, sister disavowal, and verdict must each arrive with visible carrier beats and immediate room response.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The legal answer lands through tightened chains, stopped counting, and the removal back toward the colder cells.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.biggestGap: Post-verdict urgency and Lor'themar's arrival can turn into emotional arithmetic unless bars, wall taps, torch color, road dust, and corridor cadence keep the chapter physical.

sceneContract.placeAnchor: Return to the dungeon cells and corridor outside them, with the vault still bleeding through the foundations.
sceneContract.materialAnchor: Tap patterns in stone, frost at blanket edge, bars between hands, road dust on Lor'themar, lantern glare at the corner.
sceneContract.povAnchor: Sylvanas treats outside reinforcement as corroboration only if it can survive the clock, the bars, and the palace's slower machinery.
sceneContract.dialogueAnchor: Wall-talk and corridor conversation stay attached to taps, bars, wrist-grip, and footsteps closing in.
sceneContract.exitAnchor: The chapter ends on Lor'themar's dust and the colder corridor above, then returns to the wall between Sylvanas and Jaina.
